description: Colombian neo-paramilitary group and drug cartel, currently the country's largest. Formed after the demobilization of the AUC, based in the Urabá region of Antioquia. Involved in drug trafficking, illegal mining, and racketeering, with around 6,000 members. Considered the most powerful criminal organization in Colombia as of 2021.

country_links.evidence_quote: smuggling more than 13 tons of cocaine into Honduras, Costa Rica, and Panama
country_links.context: The Clan del Golfo smuggled cocaine into Costa Rica.

country_links.evidence_quote: shared routes for drug trafficking to Europe and the United States with the Clan del Golfo
country_links.context: The Clan del Golfo shared drug trafficking routes to Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: smuggling more than 13 tons of cocaine into Honduras, Costa Rica, and Panama
country_links.context: The Clan del Golfo smuggled cocaine into Honduras.

country_links.evidence_quote: having formed direct, highly-lucrative partnerships with drug cartels in Mexico
country_links.context: The Clan del Golfo has formed direct partnerships with drug cartels in Mexico.

country_links.evidence_quote: extending only as far as the Panama border
country_links.context: The Clan del Golfo extends its operations to the Panama border and its cocaine was seized by Colombian and Panamanian security forces.

country_links.evidence_quote: senior Clan del Gulfo drug trafficker and money launderer Joaquin Guillermo David-Usuga was extradited from Colombia to the U.S. city of Houston, Texas.
country_links.context: A senior Clan del Golfo member was extradited to the U.S. city of Houston, Texas.
